English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cayuga County sugar beet industry records
Colman, Gould P. (Gould Patchin), 1926-
Bateman, Theodore
Swanson, C. L. W.
Bugbee, J. T.
3249
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1961-1979. Correspondence, Newspapers, Photographs, Printed Materials, Publications, Research Materials, Scrapbooks Photocopies of newspaper clippings concerning the Maine Sugar Industries, Inc., the Finger Lakes Sugar Beet Growers Association Collection material in English 2085466
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2010
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2010
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Photocopies of newspaper clippings concerning the Maine Sugar Industries, Inc., the Finger Lakes Sugar Beet Growers Association, and unsuccessful attempts to develop a sugar beet industry in central New York. Includes photographs, bulletin preparation materials, correspondence, notes, and a research proposal about the project. Also includes two films: "The Root of Progress" and "Introducing Sugar Beets," tables, scrapbooks, and additional correspondence, photographs, and bulletins.
Also, "Finger Lakes Sugar Beet Growers Association and Pepsi-Cola Company Present the Story of Beet Sugar. Authors: C. L. Swanson and J. T. Bugbee Through the Courtesy of Texaco's Magazine Lubrication."
NOTES
Material was collected by Gould Colman and Theodore Bateman.
Cite As:
Cayuga County sugar beet industry records, #3249. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
